title: Why Am I Not Losing Weight on Ozempic? 10 Common Plateau Reasons
introduction: If the scale is not moving on Ozempic, you are not alone. Most stalls come from fixable issues: hidden calories, low protein, poor tracking, or unrealistic expectations during normal fluctuations.

sections.heading: 🧩 Top Plateau Causes on Ozempic
sections.content:
| Cause | Fix |
| --- | --- |
| Hidden calories | Track oils, sauces, snacks, drinks |
| Appetite drift over time | Rebuild meal structure |
| Too little protein | Set daily protein target |
| No strength training | Add 2-4 sessions/week |
| Scale-only focus | Use weekly averages and measurements |

Many stalls are adherence problems, not medication failure.

sections.heading: How to Diagnose a Stall in 14 Days
sections.content:
Checklist:

1) Log every meal
2) Track protein daily
3) Keep activity steady
4) Weigh under same conditions

Useful reads:
• [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly)
• [why am I not losing weight in a calorie deficit](/blog/why-am-i-not-losing-weight-in-a-calorie-deficit)

sections.heading: When to Reassess With Your Clinician
sections.content:
Consider medical review when:

• Adherence is high but trend is flat for multiple weeks
• Side effects limit intake quality
• Dose strategy may need adjustment

Bring objective logs instead of memory-based estimates.

sections.heading: Text CTA: Replace Guessing with Data
sections.content: Text CTA: Use Eati for 2 full weeks of strict logging before changing your plan. Most plateaus become obvious once intake and protein data are visible.

midArticleCta: Track every meal in Eati for 14 days to diagnose your Ozempic plateau with real data.
conclusion: Most Ozempic stalls are solvable with better measurement and structure. Tighten tracking, protect protein, train consistently, and reassess based on weekly trends.
metaTitle: Why Am I Not Losing Weight on Ozempic? Plateau Fix Guide
metaDescription: Not losing weight on Ozempic? Learn the most common plateau causes and how to fix them with calorie tracking, protein, and training.
targetKeyword: why am i not losing weight on ozempic

faqs.question: Can you plateau on Ozempic even if appetite is low?
faqs.answer: Yes. Plateaus can still happen due to adaptation, hidden calories, lower activity, or insufficient protein and resistance training.
